[01] · creative brand strategist

foram patel. she / her

creative strategist / content lead / brand strategist /
what I do
I build culture-aware brand systems that turn content into momentum.

I help brands show up with clarity, taste, and work people want to engage with.

brand strategy campaign thinking content direction community-first ideas
positioning
Not just marketing — sharper brand thinking, stronger storytelling, and ideas built to travel.
Best fit: beauty, fashion, lifestyle, and culture-led brands with a strong creative point of view.

[02] · about (she/her)

Hi, I'm Foram. I turn brand thinking into content, campaigns, and systems people understand, remember, and act on.

I think in:

  • formats
  • hooks
  • narratives
  • consumer behavior
  • systems that perform
snapshot · 2026

[03] · what i do

what i do

01 · Brand Strategy

Who you're for, what you stand for, how you sound.

Positioning, audience, voice, and narrative — the one-line answer your team can repeat.

Positioning Voice & Tone Audience
02 · Content Strategy

What to say, when to say it, why now.

Pillars, calendars, and formats built around how your audience actually scrolls.

Pillars Calendar Formats
03 · Creative Direction

How it looks, sounds, and feels — every touchpoint.

Mood, references, art direction, and review — so the strategy survives the final cut.

Mood Art Direction References
04 · Campaign Launches

From the first scribbled idea to the post-launch read.

Concept, brief, production, launch, and review — built to move cleanly from idea to market.

Concepting Briefs Go-to-Market
05 · Social Strategy

Systems for showing up — and being remembered.

Channel playbooks, hooks, frequency, and editorial logic that make the brand easier to remember.

Roadmap Hooks Editorial
06 · Shoot Direction

On set, in the room — making the brief look like the brief.

Pre-pro, references, shot lists, and on-set direction that keep every frame on-brief.

Pre-Pro Shot Lists On-Set
⊹ Flagship · End-to-End
Ideation → Production → Launch → Performance
Or run it end to end.
⌗
[04] · how i work

how i work

Before the deck, I understand four things — so the work is built with intent, not just to fill the calendar.

↳ four things first.
01

the brand

Founders, history, values, and the gap between how the brand sees itself and how the world sees it.

02

the audience

Who they are, what they save, what they share, and what they scroll past. Not personas. Behaviour.

03

what's working
(and what isn't)

An honest audit of what is working, what is noise, and what looked good but did not move anyone.

04

trend forecasting

Where the category is going, what to act on, and which trends to ignore on purpose.

From there, everything is built to work — not just to fill content.

[07] · toolkit

the things on my desktop

strategy & research

where the thinking happens
Notion FigJam Google Suite Airtable SEMrush Search Console Trends + cultural reading

Brand audits, audience research, content audits, competitive scans, trend forecasting, performance reviews — and the long Notion docs nobody reads but everyone references three months in.

craft & direction

where the thinking gets made
Figma Canva Adobe Suite CapCut Lightroom Sprout Social Hootsuite Later / Buffer Keynote Pinterest mood boards (unironically)

Decks, content calendars, social roadmaps, shoot briefs, reference boards, on-set direction — the stuff that turns a strategy into something you can actually post on Tuesday morning.

performance & paid

where it gets tracked
GA4 Meta Ads LinkedIn Ads Google Ads HubSpot Mailchimp Looker Studio Shopify Amazon Seller Central

Paid media, lifecycle automation, attribution, customer journey work — where the campaign meets a dashboard and the strategy gets a feedback loop. The numbers that decide if any of it actually worked.
